methods.description: Process of collecting various types of subsurface data from exploration and appraisal wells targeting Dacian gas-bearing reservoirs, including wireline logs, formation tester data, core data, and XRD analyses.

steps.description: Collected wireline geophysical logs, including total gamma ray, apparent resistivity (Dual Laterolog, Laterolog Array and focused microresistivity tools), neutron porosity, litho-density (bulk density and photoelectric factor) and compressional sonic transit time. For some of the recently drilled wells, electrical imaging (FMI -Fullbore Formation Microimager) and nuclear magnetic resonance (CMR -Combinable Magnetic Resonance Tool) logs were also available.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect wireline geophysical logs from wells.
outputs: Wireline log data (GR, resistivity, neutron, density, sonic, FMI, CMR)

steps.description: Collected formation tester pressure measurements and fluid samples (RFT -Repeat Formation Tester and MDT -Modular Formation Dynamics Tester).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect formation tester pressure measurements and fluid samples.

steps.description: Collected conventional and special laboratory petrophysical measurements on cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect laboratory petrophysical measurements on cores.
outputs: Core analysis data (petrophysical properties, Archie parameters, matrix density)

steps.searchable_summary: Collect X-ray diffraction analysis data on cores.

methods.description: Analysis of pressure measurements and fluid samples obtained from wireline formation testers to determine reservoir pressure, fluid composition, and density.

steps.description: Analyzed 105 successful pressure measurements carried out with wireline formation testers in the wells from Field A and Field B.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze formation pressure measurements to determine reservoir pressure ranges.

steps.description: Analyzed gas samples collected during wireline testing operations in three wells drilled in these fields.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze gas samples to determine composition (methane, H2S).

steps.description: Calculated gas density at the reservoirs pressure and temperature (38 -40 °C, measured in the wireline logging and formation tester runs).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate gas density at reservoir pressure and temperature.

methods.description: Analysis of core samples using X-ray diffraction to determine total clay content and identify clay mineral types.

steps.description: Performed XRD analyses on 16 core samples extracted from the "Sand" reservoir facies of Field A.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform X-ray diffraction analysis on core samples.

steps.description: Determined total clay content ranging from 13% to 53% by weight (29% on average).
steps.searchable_summary: Process XRD data to determine total clay content by weight.

steps.description: Identified the clays present as predominantly illite + mica, mixed-layer illite/smectite and chlorite, with only subordinate kaolinite observed.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze XRD data to identify and quantify clay mineral types.
outputs: Clay mineral types (illite+mica, mixed-layer illite/smectite, chlorite, kaolinite) and relative proportions

methods.method_name: Wireline Log Data Processing and Interpretation
methods.description: Detailed processing and interpretation of wireline geophysical logs using petrophysical models appropriate for shaly sands to derive reservoir properties like clay volume, porosity, water saturation, and fluid volumes.

steps.description: Clay volume (Vclay) estimation by using a combination of gamma ray and density-neutron clay indicators. A linear clay volume estimated from the GR log (Vclay,GR) was calibrated against a clay volume estimated from the density-neutron crossplot (Vclay,DN) in order to obtain Vclay,GR ≈ Vclay,DN ≈ 1 (100%) at the depth levels with good hole conditions and a maximum and consistent separation between the neutron and density curves (ϕN -ϕD) on a sandstone compatible porosity scale. The corresponding expressions are ... (formulas provided in text). Once calibrated against Vclay,DN on clay/shale or water-bearing intervals, Vclay,GR can be used as a valid clay volume estimator for the entire interpreted intervals, because it is not affected by light hydrocarbon (gas) effects.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate clay volume from gamma ray and density-neutron logs, calibrate gamma ray estimate using density-neutron estimate in non-gas zones.

steps.description: Effective porosity (ϕ) estimation by using a density-neutron crossplot porosity model appropriate for shaly sands. The matrix (grain) densities δma of 2.67 -2.69 g/cm 3 used in the calculations were obtained from measurements on core plugs. The clay parameters were statistically selected from the logs, resulting neutron porosities ϕNclay = 0.45 -0.50 and densities δclay = 2.25 -2.26 g/cm 3 .
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate effective porosity using a density-neutron crossplot model for shaly sands.

steps.description: Estimation of formation water resistivity (ρw) by using effective porosity vs. true resistivity Pickett crossplots [6], i.e. log(ϕ) = f(log(ρt)), and approximating ρt with the deepest investigation apparent resistivity curves RLA5 (Fig. 4). For the wells of Field A we obtained ρw = 0.5 -0.6 Ω·m at formations temperature, corresponding to salinities of 6600 -9100 ppm equivalent NaCl. For the wells of Field B we obtained ρw = 0.9 -1.2 Ω·m at formations temperature, corresponding to salinities of 3400 -4700 ppm equivalent NaCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate formation water resistivity using Pickett crossplots of effective porosity and deep resistivity.

steps.description: Estimation of water saturations (Sw, Sxo) and total, movable and residual hydrocarbon saturations (Sh, Shm, Shr) in the reservoir intervals by using a shaly sands saturation model [7], in the effective porosity system. Clay resistivities were statistically selected from the logs (ρclay = 5.2 -8.5 Ω·m) and the water saturations were computed using Archie parameters [8] measured on core plugs, at a confining pressure representative for reservoir conditions. For Field A the average values of these parameters were a = 1.0 (the socalled tortuosity factor), m = 1.71 (cementation exponent), n = 1.67 (saturation exponent) and for Field B the average parameters were a = 1.0, m = 1.54, n = 1.33.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate water and hydrocarbon saturations using a shaly sands model and Archie parameters.

steps.description: Processing the CMR T2 transverse relaxation time distribution, in order to compute claybound water, capillary-bound water and free fluid volumes (T2 cutoffs of 3 ms and 33 ms, typical for sands / sandstones, were used to separate the three types of pore fluids), as well as a CMR-derived water saturation.
steps.searchable_summary: Process CMR T2 distribution to calculate fluid volumes (clay-bound, capillary-bound, free fluid) and CMR-derived water saturation using T2 cutoffs.

steps.description: Linear regression analysis on the good quality formation pressure data, in order to derive the pressure gradients (∆p/∆H, where p -measured formations pressure, H -true vertical depth), the formation fluids in-situ densities and to identify the probable gas-water contacts (GWC).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform linear regression on formation pressure vs. depth data to calculate pressure gradients, fluid densities, and identify gas-water contacts.
